name: parallel-review
description: Three reader agents review the diff in parallel from different angles (correctness / perf / security), then a synthesizer agent merges findings into a single report.

nodes.user:
Review the working-tree changes (`git diff HEAD --stat` then `git diff HEAD`) ONLY through the **correctness** lens: missing edge cases, off-by-one, contract violations, broken invariants. Output a short markdown report under .agent-loop/reviews/correctness.md. End with the line VERDICT: PASS or VERDICT: FAIL.

User intent: {{input.prompt}}

Review the working-tree changes ONLY through the **performance** lens: O(n) regressions, N+1 queries, unnecessary allocations, blocking I/O on hot paths. Output a short markdown report under .agent-loop/reviews/perf.md. End with VERDICT: PASS or VERDICT: FAIL.

User intent: {{input.prompt}}

Review the working-tree changes ONLY through the **security** lens: input validation, secrets, injection, authn/authz, path traversal, unsafe deserialization. Output .agent-loop/reviews/security.md. End with VERDICT: PASS or VERDICT: FAIL.

User intent: {{input.prompt}}

Three reviewers wrote individual reports under .agent-loop/reviews/ (correctness.md, perf.md, security.md). Read all three, deduplicate, prioritize, and write a single consolidated report to .agent-loop/reviews/_summary.md. Pull each finding's severity (blocker / warning / note) and group by file. End the summary with one of: OVERALL: PASS or OVERALL: FAIL.
